title: PluginManager.install_from_marketplace: 160-line function violates SRP
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension**: Structural Modularity (SRP / Cyclomatic complexity)

**Location**: src/plugins/manager.rs:457-618 (install_from_marketplace — 160 lines, marked with #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)])

**Violation**: This function is explicitly annotated `#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]` with the hand-waving comment 'Complex installer, splitting would reduce readability'. Per CLAUDE.md global instructions (NEVER suppress lint warnings, fix the root cause) this allow is a smell. The function does:
1. Lookup marketplace
2. Lookup plugin in marketplace
3. Validate plugin name for traversal
4. Check destination exists
5. Dispatch on PluginSource variant
6. For Path: canonicalize, verify containment, copy
7. For Structured(Url|GitHub): git clone, track, save, reload
8. For Structured(Npm|Pip): error
9. Re-track, re-save, re-reload for Path case

The structured-Url/GitHub branch does a FULL install+track+return INSIDE the match arm (manager.rs:537-582), duplicating tracking/save/reload logic with the path branch (manager.rs:589-614). DRY violation — identical 20-line block in two places.

Cyclomatic complexity is >15 (multiple match arms, nested error handling, early returns).

**Mandated Refactor**:
1. Extract private helpers:
   - fn prepare_dest_dir(plugin_name: &str) -> Result<PathBuf>
   - fn fetch_source(&self, mp_plugin: &MarketplacePlugin, marketplace_dir: &Path, dest: &Path) -> Result<()> that handles ALL PluginSource variants
   - fn track_install(&mut self, plugin_id: &str, dest: &Path, version: Option<String>) -> Result<()>
2. install_from_marketplace becomes ~30 lines calling the three helpers.
3. Remove the `#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]`.
4. The duplicated InstalledPlugins::load+upsert+save+reload pattern moves into track_install, invoked once.
